scoreMeta.label: Working baseline
scoreMeta.note: AnnualCreditReport PDFs do not include true FICO scores. Replace this placeholder with myFICO and SmartCredit as soon as monitoring is active.

bureauReports.summary: Most cluttered file of the three. It shows the core collections and charge-offs plus Experian-only live pain like StellarFinance and the I C System collection.
highlights: Verizon, Midland, Sunrise, Merrick, First Premier, Perpay, Capital One all report here
Only bureau in this stack clearly showing StellarFinance as a live past-due line
Carries older derogatory history from Austin Capital, Brigit, and Sezzle

bureauReports.summary: Confirms the core derogatory set and current balances on Capital One and Perpay. It also surfaces legacy late-trade damage like Austin Capital, Brigit, and Carmax.
highlights: Confirms Verizon, Midland, Merrick, First Premier, Perpay, Sunrise, and Capital One
Does not show I C System or StellarFinance in this PDF
Shows Carmax auto late history that can drag approvals even though the loan is current

bureauReports.summary: Confirms Verizon, I C Systems, Midland, Sunrise, Merrick, First Premier, Perpay, and Capital One while also carrying old late-trade damage from Brigit, Austin Capital, Sezzle, and Carmax.
highlights: Confirms the I C Systems Comcast collection that Equifax does not show
Shows Perpay at 120 days past due and Capital One as current with heavy historical lates
Carries legacy late marks from Austin Capital, Brigit, Sezzle, and Carmax

blockers.nextStep: Build one clean validation and identity packet, then send it only when you are ready to track mailing proof and response deadlines.
blockers.notes: All three bureaus still show this as a live collection. Prior dispute language appears in bureau data, but your SSOT should treat this as not worked until you have your own proof trail.

blockers.nextStep: Open a validation case first, confirm chain-of-title from Credit One, and decide whether this is a dispute-first or settlement-first file.
blockers.notes: This is a three-bureau debt buyer collection and one of the biggest score suppressors on the file.

blockers.nextStep: Pull service dates, address history, and utility records so you can challenge ownership or settle from a position of facts.
blockers.notes: This utility collection is confirmed on all three reports and should be handled in the first dispute wave with Verizon and Midland.

blockers.nextStep: Because the balance is tiny, choose fast written validation or a written pay-for-delete style settlement attempt before wasting cycles.
blockers.notes: This one is not showing on Equifax, which makes it a cleaner targeted cleanup candidate.

blockers.nextStep: Decide if this is a validation, settlement, or age-and-monitor file, but do not leave it unplanned because it is reporting across all three.
blockers.notes: Three-bureau charge-off with long severe delinquency history. It is one of the biggest approval killers on the file.

blockers.nextStep: Pair this with Merrick in the charge-off strategy lane so both accounts get a written decision instead of sitting unresolved.
blockers.notes: Another three-bureau charge-off. Smaller than Merrick, but still toxic because it confirms repeated revolving default behavior.

blockers.nextStep: Get this current immediately or secure a written hardship/payment arrangement before it becomes another charge-off.
blockers.notes: All three bureaus show live delinquency. The total balance is $727, but the immediate cure amount is the $135 past due.

blockers.nextStep: Treat this as a same-week cash triage item. Bring it current or settle it before layering on any new credit strategy.
blockers.notes: Experian is the only bureau clearly reporting this live past-due line, but one active delinquency is enough to derail near-term approvals.

blockers.nextStep: Push utilization below 30% of the $500 limit immediately, then below 10% when possible, and do not miss another payment.
blockers.notes: The account is current now, but all three bureaus still reflect severe historical delinquencies and over-limit behavior. This is the live revolving control problem in the file.

legacyIssues.issue: Closed secured loan that still reports 30/60-day late history from early 2025.
legacyIssues.strategy: Lower priority than live collections. Revisit as a goodwill or accuracy-cleanup project after the current negatives are stabilized.

legacyIssues.creditor: Brigit / Coastal Community Bank / Bridge It
legacyIssues.issue: Paid and closed, but the file still carries late-payment history from 2025.
legacyIssues.strategy: Archive as a legacy late mark and only chase cleanup after the live collections, charge-offs, and current delinquencies are under control.

legacyIssues.issue: Current auto loan with prior 30/30/60/60 late history in 2025.
legacyIssues.strategy: Keep perfect going forward and avoid piling on new leverage while this seasoning period is still fresh.

legacyIssues.issue: Zero-balance line that still carries heavy 2021-2022 late history.
legacyIssues.strategy: No immediate action needed, but do not add new BNPL exposure while rebuilding.

stackChecklist.name: Monarch Money
stackChecklist.requirement: Needs complete account linking and a weekly review habit.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Connect checking, savings, Capital One, Perpay, Carmax, and every live liability this week.

stackChecklist.name: Credit Versio
stackChecklist.requirement: Needs a clean three-bureau case inventory and dispute calendar.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Create cases for Verizon, Midland, Sunrise, I C Systems, Merrick, and First Premier.

stackChecklist.name: myFICO
stackChecklist.requirement: Needed for real FICO baselines and lender-grade score tracking.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Buy the monitoring plan and log all mortgage, auto, and card scores monthly.

stackChecklist.name: SmartCredit
stackChecklist.requirement: Needed for weekly pulse checks between myFICO pulls.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Use it for weekly bureau monitoring, but treat myFICO as the score authority.

stackChecklist.name: Ally Emergency Vault
stackChecklist.requirement: Needs a dedicated savings bucket and automatic transfers.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Open the vault and fund the first month of runway before chasing premium cards.

stackChecklist.name: Fidelity Core / SPAXX
stackChecklist.requirement: Needs a clean base brokerage and cash management workflow.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Open the base account and define SPAXX as the parking layer for investable cash.

stackChecklist.name: Roth IRA
stackChecklist.requirement: Emergency fund and monthly free cash flow need to exist first.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Open the shell now if you want, but only automate contributions after cash triage is stable.

stackChecklist.name: Solo 401(k)
stackChecklist.requirement: Requires stable self-employment income and tax process discipline.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Set this up after Monarch, emergency reserves, and Fidelity base workflows are stable.

stackChecklist.name: Phantom Vault Wallet
stackChecklist.requirement: Only useful after cash reserves and taxes are under control.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Create the wallet after the cash safety layer is live, not before.

stackChecklist.name: Kamino Yield Layer
stackChecklist.requirement: Needs surplus cash, risk budget, and a non-chaotic core financial system.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Do not touch this until disputes, delinquent accounts, and emergency reserves are stable.

stackChecklist.name: Apple Card
stackChecklist.requirement: Usually wants a cleaner recent file and roughly 660+ real FICO.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Hold until collections and live delinquencies stop dragging the file and you have three clean cycles.

stackChecklist.name: Amazon Prime Visa
stackChecklist.requirement: Usually wants a cleaner recent file and roughly 660+ real FICO.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Treat this the same as Apple Card: no application until the file is materially cleaner.

stackChecklist.name: Fidelity Visa
stackChecklist.requirement: Usually wants 690+ and recent clean payment behavior.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Target this only after the low-tier cards are realistically winnable.

stackChecklist.name: Amex Gold
stackChecklist.requirement: Usually wants 690+ with far cleaner recent behavior than the current file shows.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Keep it aspirational until the collections and charge-offs are no longer defining the profile.

stackChecklist.name: Chase Private Client Hub
stackChecklist.requirement: Needs stable cash flow and organized routing, not just a headline status goal.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Build regular checking and savings discipline first, then upgrade when balances and inflows support it.

stackChecklist.name: Amex Platinum
stackChecklist.requirement: Best after the core card stack and cash flow layers are already clean and productive.
stackChecklist.nextStep: Treat this as a final-stage upgrade, not an early rebuild tool.

actionPlan.title: Truth and Monitoring Baseline
actionPlan.description: Stop guessing. Build the monitoring and systems layer that turns this into a real operating dashboard.

items.task: Activate myFICO and log your real mortgage, auto, and bankcard scores.
items.why: The annual credit reports do not include scores, so you still need a real baseline before timing applications.

items.task: Activate SmartCredit for weekly three-bureau score and alert checks.
items.why: You need a lighter-weight weekly pulse between myFICO check-ins.

items.task: Build Monarch Money as the actual command center and link every active bank, card, loan, and cash bucket.
items.why: Your stack only works if cash flow, debt balances, and reserves live in one place.

items.task: Create the three-bureau tradeline register in Credit Versio or a matching case tracker before sending any disputes.
items.why: Without bureau-by-bureau case tracking you will lose deadlines, evidence, and version control.

actionPlan.title: Immediate Live Account Triage
actionPlan.description: Fix the accounts that are currently capable of getting worse before touching prestige-stack applications.

items.task: Bring Perpay current or secure a written hardship/payment arrangement.
items.why: This is a live three-bureau delinquency and can easily turn into another permanent derogatory.

items.task: Bring StellarFinance current or settle it immediately.
items.why: It is still a live delinquency and should be treated like an emergency clean-up item.

items.task: Pay Capital One below 30% of the $500 limit, then target AZEO once cash triage stabilizes.
items.why: The account is current, but utilization and historical lates still suppress approvals.

items.task: Put Carmax on autopay with a one-payment cash buffer so no more auto-loan lates hit the file.
items.why: A current auto loan with fresh 30/60 history can still kill near-term underwriting.

actionPlan.title: Collections and Charge-Off Attack Plan
actionPlan.description: Hit the biggest score suppressors in a deliberate order instead of random dispute spam.

items.task: Build the Verizon validation packet with service dates, address history, and identity proof.
items.why: This is the largest three-bureau collection on the file.

items.task: Build the Midland debt-buyer packet and confirm its chain from Credit One before negotiating anything.
items.why: Debt-buyer collections are often where documentation gaps exist.

items.task: Pull the underlying utility history for Sunrise so you can challenge ownership or settle from facts.
items.why: A utility collection with no prep work is easy to mishandle.

items.task: Choose a fast strategy for I C Systems: validation first or written pay-for-delete attempt because the balance is only $50.
items.why: This is the smallest collection and should not consume the same energy as Verizon or Midland.

items.task: Write a resolution decision memo for Merrick and First Premier: dispute, settle, or let them age while you focus on live accounts.
items.why: Two revolving charge-offs across all three bureaus are enough to kill premium-card timing.

actionPlan.title: 10/10 Stack Infrastructure
actionPlan.description: Build the systems that should exist regardless of your score today.

items.task: Open and label the Ally emergency vault, then fund month one of reserves.
items.why: Cash safety has to come before premium leverage.

items.task: Open the Fidelity base account and define the SPAXX parking workflow.
items.why: This is the clean foundation for the investing side of the stack.

items.task: Set a weekly 15-minute Monarch review and a monthly SSOT credit review on the same day every month.
items.why: The stack only becomes real when the cadence is automatic.

items.task: Organize your dispute vault: one folder per tradeline, one log per bureau, zero fake docs until real mail or evidence exists.
items.why: You asked for a true SSOT, so the evidence layer must stay clean and honest.

actionPlan.title: Sequence the Stack Unlocks
actionPlan.description: Do not apply for shiny stuff out of order. Earn the unlocks in a way that actually sticks.

items.task: Do not apply for any new consumer cards until Perpay and Stellar are current and you have at least three clean reporting cycles.
items.why: New inquiries on top of current delinquencies is self-sabotage.

items.task: Target Apple Card and Amazon Prime Visa only after real FICO tracking shows you are truly near the 660 band.
items.why: These are the first realistic consumer card unlocks, not the starting point.

items.task: Target Fidelity Visa and Amex Gold only after the charge-offs are no longer the dominant story on the file and real FICO is near 690.
items.why: Premium cards should be the reward for cleanup, not the motivation to skip it.

items.task: Treat Chase Private Client, Amex Platinum, and the Kamino yield layer as final-stage upgrades after cash flow, reserves, and credit stability are real.
items.why: Status products are worthless if the underlying file and cash system are still unstable.

custodianLog.message: Rebuilt the SSOT from all three Mar 2026 bureau reports: Experian, Equifax, and TransUnion.
Removed fake dispute documents, removed fake completed actions, and reset the action engine to zero completed work.
Kept the score ring as a working placeholder only. Real score tracking still needs myFICO and SmartCredit because the bureau PDFs do not include FICO scores.
